description_long: This cowshed consists of two adjoining rectangular buildings, each with a distinct roof style and weathered exterior. The larger structure features a long, low profile with a reddish corrugated metal roof and multiple small, darkened windows set into rough, aged masonry walls. The smaller building has a grayish gable roof with visible ridge lines and a similar stone facade, punctuated by several rectangular windows and a central door. Both buildings show signs of age and utilitarian construction, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function. The doors and windows are simple, likely wooden or metal, and the overall appearance suggests a structure designed for livestock shelter and agricultural use. The muted color palette and robust materials indicate durability and practicality, suitable for rural or peri-urban environments.
description: A pair of adjoining, weathered stone cowsheds with corrugated metal and gable roofs, featuring simple doors and small, dark windows. The structures are utilitarian, robust, and show signs of age.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the long side of the larger cowshed with its red corrugated roof and several small windows, alongside the gable-roofed smaller building.
description_view_1: View 1 highlights the end of the larger building and the full side of the smaller building, showing the gray gable roof and more window placements.
description_view_2: View 2 presents the opposite long side of the larger cowshed and the side of the smaller building, emphasizing the stonework and door placement.
description_view_3: View 3 focuses on the rear of the smaller building and the end of the larger building, with the red roof and stone walls clearly visible.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door, utilitarian design
attribute_window_presence: multiple rectangular windows on all sides, small and simple
